methodology_sections: 1. Conservation Laws - Solvency, inflation checks, supply integrity
2. Relationship & Ratio Invariants - Collateralization, rate consistency
3. Economic Monotonicity - Yield accumulation, exchange rate direction
4. Incentive & Game Theoretic Integrity - Attack cost vs profit, slashing
5. Fee & Yield Consistency - Fee accounting, yield distribution

invariants.description: Cost basis is strictly conserved across all share transfers and state transitions
invariants.attack_scenario: If cost basis is lost or artificially inflated during transfers or partial withdrawals, users could avoid paying performance fees on their profits or be unfairly charged fees on their principal.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_costBasisConservation() public view { /* sum userBasis and queued bases, compare to historical fulfilled deposits */ }

invariants.description: Performance fee and user payout strictly equal the allocated withdrawal assets
invariants.attack_scenario: Fee miscalculation could lead to the vault paying out more assets than allocated (causing insolvency) or the user receiving less than their fair share minus fees (yield theft).
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_feeConservation(uint256 epoch, address user) public { uint256 preBalanceUser = token.balanceOf(user); uint256 preBalanceFee = token.balanceOf(feeWallet); executeWithdrawal(user, epoch); assert((token.balanceOf(user) - preBalanceUser) + (token.balanceOf(feeWallet) - preBalanceFee) == calculated_assetsToWithdraw); }

invariants.description: Total supply strictly tracks fulfilled epochs, independent of asynchronous user execution
invariants.attack_scenario: If totalSupply deviates from the sum of fulfilled deposits minus fulfilled withdrawals, the share price (NAV / totalSupply) will be manipulated, allowing attackers to extract value during fulfillment.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_totalSupply() public view { assert(totalSupply == 1_000_000 + totalSharesReceived - totalSharesFulfilled); }

invariants.description: Fulfilled amounts cannot exceed requested amounts in any epoch
invariants.attack_scenario: If the manager fulfills more assets/shares than requested, it creates unbacked shares or allocates non-existent assets, diluting users and causing vault insolvency.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_fulfillmentBounds(uint256 e) public view { assert(depositEpochState[e].assetsFulfilled <= depositEpochState[e].assetsDeposited); assert(withdrawalEpochState[e].sharesFulfilled <= withdrawalEpochState[e].sharesWithdrawn); }

invariants.description: Reserved withdrawal assets must be greater than or equal to the sum of unexecuted fulfilled withdrawals
invariants.attack_scenario: If reservedWithdrawalAssets is less than the unexecuted fulfilled withdrawals, the vault will revert when users try to execute their withdrawals, trapping their funds.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_reservedAssets() public view { assert(reservedWithdrawalAssets >= totalAssetsReceived - totalExecutedAssets); }

invariants.description: Past epoch states are strictly immutable once fulfilled
invariants.attack_scenario: If past epochs can be modified, the basis for pending user executions changes, allowing theft of shares or assets from users who have not yet called executeDeposit/executeWithdrawal.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_epochImmutability() public view { /* verify past epochs match stored historical hashes */ }

invariants.description: Manager-provided prices must strictly adhere to TWAP oracle bounds
invariants.attack_scenario: If the manager could provide arbitrary prices bypassing the maxPriceDeviation check, they could artificially inflate or deflate the NAV during fulfillment, stealing value from depositors or withdrawers.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_oracleBounds() public view { /* Enforced by revert StaleOraclePrice() */ }
